---
title: Running pySCENIC on UBC subclusters
date: 2025-03-19
metadata-files:
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To run pySCENIC on the integrated human UBCs and infer the active GRNs in each cluster.


## Files and directories

### Code

The code was run by submitting the a bash script as a job to the HPC cluster. This script can be found in the output results folder (named `human_ubc_pyscenic.sh`).

Briefly, this bash script runs two downstream scripts:

1. `<pyscenic software directory>/seurat_to_scenic.R` extracts the Seurat counts, metadata, and UMAP embeddings from the integrated human UBC dataset from Quant (this Seurat object is located at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/software/pyscenic/seurat_to_scenic.R`).
2. The Python script at `HumanMouseUBC/integrated_human_ubc/03_pyscenic.py` was used to run pySCENIC.

::: {.callout-note}
# Conda environments

Note that step 1 was run using the `scrnaseq_env` conda environment and step 2 was run using the `pyscenic` conda environment.
:::

### Output

The output from the two code files are as follows:

1. `seurat_to_scenic.R` outputs the Seurat counts, metadata, and UMAP embeddings as CSV files
2. `03_pyscenic.py` outputs the remaining files and figures from pySCENIC

The output can be found in `<pailab projects>/HumanMouseUBC/integrated_human_ubc/20250313/`. 


## Results

::: {.callout-note}
The figures here are just a few of the figures generated using pySCENIC. All other figures are saved in the output directory as noted above.
:::

::: {#fig-umap}
![](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/integrated_human_ubc/20250319/umap.png)

UMAPs generated by `scanpy`/pySCENIC in Python. Cells are coloured by (A) clusters as annotated by Quang, (B) dataset, and (C) age of the cells.
:::

::: {#fig-rss}
![](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/integrated_human_ubc/20250319/regulon_specificity_scores.png)

Regulon specificity scores of the regulons inferred by pySCENIC. The top eight regulons for each UBC cluster are highlighted and labelled.
:::
